Do what thou wilt shall be the whole of the Law.
Every man and every woman is a star.
Since the Law is Do what thou wilt, let there be no other Law besides.
The word of Sin is Restriction.
There is no law beyond Do what thou wilt.
Love is the law, love under will.
For pure will, unassuaged of purpose, delivered from the lust of result, is every way perfect.
Thou hast no right but to do thy will. Do that, and no other shall say nay.
Every number is infinite; there is no difference.
Be strong, o man! lust, enjoy all things of sense and rapture: fear not that any God shall deny thee for this.
I am the Hawk-Headed Lord of Silence and of Strength; my nemyss shrouds the night-blue sky.
I am the Snake that giveth Knowledge and Delight and bright glory, and stir the hearts of men with drunkenness.
Come! all ye, and learn the secret that hath not yet been revealed. I, Hadit, am the complement of Nu, my bride.
In the sphere I am everywhere the centre, as she, the circumference, is nowhere found.
The Perfect and the Perfect are one Perfect and not two; nay, are none!
Nothing is a secret key of this law. Sixty-one the Jews call it; I call it eight, eighty, four hundred and eighteen.
But to love me is better than all things: if under the night-stars in the desert thou presently burnest mine incense before me, invoking me with a pure heart, and the Serpent flame therein, thou shalt come a little to lie in my bosom.
I am above you and in you. My ecstasy is in yours. My joy is to see your joy.
Now ye shall know that the chosen priest and apostle of infinite space is the prince-priest the Beast; and in his woman called the Scarlet Woman is all power given.
Beauty and strength, leaping laughter and delicious languor, force and fire, are of us.
Think not, o king, upon that lie: That Thou Must Die: verily thou shalt not die, but live.
Wisdom says: be strong! Then canst thou bear more joy.
Remember all ye that existence is pure joy; that all the sorrows are but as shadows; they pass and are done; but there is that which remains.
There is no bond that can unite the divided but love: all else is a curse.
The word of the Law is Thelema.
Who calls us Thelemites will do no wrong, if he look but close into the word. For there are therein Three Grades, the Hermit, and the Lover, and the man of Earth.
Let my servants be few and secret: they shall rule the many and the known.
I am the warrior Lord of the Forties: the Eighties cower before me, and are abased.
Choose ye an island! Fortify it! Dung it about with enginery of war!
The study of this Book is forbidden. It is wise to destroy this copy after the first reading.
The fall of Because, is the fall of all. Let there be no other reason than Will.
Enough of Because! Be he damned for a dog!
Is a God to live in a dog? No! but the highest are of us.
Ah! Ah! Death! Death! thou shalt long for death. Death is forbidden, o man, unto thee.
There is a veil: that veil is black. It is the veil of the modest woman; it is the veil of sorrow, and the pall of death: this is none of me. Tear down that lying spectre of the centuries: veil not your vices in virtuous words: these vices are my service; ye do well, and I will reward you here and hereafter.
Dost thou fail? Art thou sorry? Is fear in thine heart? Where I am these are not.
Unity uttermost showed! I adore the might of Thy breath, Supreme and terrible God, Who makest the gods and death to tremble before Thee: I, I adore thee!
The ending of the words is the Word Abrahadabra.
